steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The company starts with 600 customers in March 2019 ($m=0$) and grows 6% per month. A 6% monthly increase means each month's count is multiplied by $1.06$. The model is $c = 600(1.06)^m$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $c = 600(0.06)^m$: $0.06$ is just the rate, not the growth factor; this decays to zero. ✗
  • Choice B — $c = 600(1.06)^m$: starting value 600, growth factor $1 + 0.06 = 1.06$, exponent $m$ months. ✓
  • Choice C — $c = 600(1.6)^m$: uses a growth factor of 1.6, which would mean a 60% monthly increase, not 6%. ✗
  • Choice D — $c = 600(6)^m$: uses a growth factor of 6, which is far larger than a 6% increase. ✗
That matches Choice B — $c = 600(1.06)^m$.
